title: Browser Origins, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Fields · Go Semantics Case: Origin Site Cors Boundary
summary: A browser API reflects arbitrary Origin with credentials, mutates state on GET, accepts ambient cookies cross-site, caches preflight incorrectly, and treats CORS as server authorization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo origin site cors boundary increment in browser request trust and cross-origin policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, CORS read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization.
Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 consistently, limit side channels, record privacy-safe decision evidence, and test cross-tenant changed cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Origin Site Cors Boundary · Theory Origin Site Cors Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4583 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack.
